line: # Rebase 8f7e6d5 onto 1029384; realistic long todo with verification hooks.
# Lines beginning with # are instructions or review notes from the editor.
# p, pick = use commit; r, reword = use commit, but edit the commit message
# e, edit = use commit, but stop for amending; s, squash = combine commits
# f, fixup = combine while discarding this message; d, drop = remove commit
# x, exec = run a shell command; b, break = stop here and continue later
p 0000002 add license and security policy
p 0000011 retain checkpoint state across blank lines
exec test -f crates/mark-syntax/Cargo.toml && cargo test -p mark-syntax
# Documentation subjects intentionally contain non-ASCII text.
pick 0000020 add résumé example for naïve input
p 0000021 document café paths in Zürich
reword 0000022 explain 東京 deployment notes
r 0000023 preserve Ελληνικά identifiers in output
f 0000029 retain astral rocket 🚀 in changelog
exec printf '%s\n' 'Unicode smoke: 東京 — مرحبًا — 🚀'
# Pause after the Unicode-sensitive changes for an interactive check.
exec env LC_ALL=C cargo test -q config::tests
# CLI series with shell pipelines and redirections.
# Drop two superseded experiments rather than replaying them.
# Quoting, substitutions, conditionals, and loops exercise embedded shell.
x if test -n "${HOME:-}"; then printf 'home=%s\n' "$HOME"; fi
exec for crate in mark-cli mark-syntax; do cargo check -q -p "$crate" || exit; done
# Leading whitespace is accepted on comments and commands by this grammar.
pick 0000090 update release notes for version 1.0
p 0000091 thank translators: Chloé, 李雷, and أمينة
f 0000099 remove release checklist scratch file
# Stop once more before publishing, then finish the todo.
# End of stress interactive-rebase fixture; trailing newline is intentional.
